notes.summary: A consumer is a person who buys goods or services for personal use. Consumers are an important part of the market system.
notes.importance: Helps students understand who is protected under consumer laws.

notes.summary: Consumer exploitation happens when sellers cheat buyers by overcharging, selling defective goods, or giving false information.
notes.importance: Creates awareness about unfair trade practices.

notes.summary: Consumers have rights such as Right to Safety, Right to Information, Right to Choose, Right to Seek Redressal and Right to Consumer Education.
notes.importance: Ensures protection and fair treatment in the market.

notes.summary: The Consumer Protection Act was passed to protect consumers from unfair practices and provide legal remedies.
Consumer courts are special courts that solve consumer complaints. There are three levels: District, State and National.
Quality marks like ISI, Agmark and Hallmark ensure that products meet safety and quality standards.
notes.importance: Helps consumers make safe purchasing decisions.
